Title: Lycium barbarum Glycopeptide Alleviates Neomycin-Induced Ototoxicity by Inhibiting Tryptophan Hydroxylase-Mediated Serotonin Biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates L-tryptophan to 5-hydroxy-L-tryptophan, initiating serotonin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established by the paper as the rate-limiting enzyme in 5-HT synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: amine neurotransmitter derived from amino acid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Neurotransmitter; in this study elevated 5-HT aggravates neomycin-induced cochlear injury and hearing dysfunction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher Tph2 expression promotes 5-HT accumulation and exacerbates neomycin-induced ototoxicity; pharmacological inhibition or AAV-mediated knockdown of Tph2 reduces hearing loss and preserves cochlear hair cells and spiral ganglion neurons
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Nontargeted metabolomics identified tryptophan metabolism as a major LBGP-responsive pathway in serum and cochlea. 2. Tph2, but not Tph1, was detected in the mouse cochlea and was upregulated by neomycin in cochlear hair cells and SGNs. 3. LBGP suppressed neomycin-induced Tph2 expression and reduced 5-HT levels in serum and cochlea. 4. Exogenous 5-HT aggravated neomycin-induced hearing impairment. 5. Pharmacological Tph inhibition with PCPA and AAV-ie-ShTph2 knockdown both partially rescued hearing and cell survival after neomycin exposure.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Serum and cochlea, including cochlear hair cells and spiral ganglion neuron-associated tissue
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in mouse cochlea; elevated in cochlear hair cells and spiral ganglion neurons after neomycin exposure; reduced by LBGP treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in organ of Corti/cochlear hair cells and SGNs; induced by neomycin; suppressed by LBGP; knocked down by AAV-ie-ShTph2 in neonatal cochlea
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Neomycin-induced ototoxicity; furosemide co-treatment in acute model; LBGP pretreatment; systemic 5-HT administration; PCPA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Nontargeted metabolomics; immunostaining of Tph2; ELISA for 5-HT; pharmacological inhibition with PCPA in vivo and explants; AAV-ie-ShTph2 knockdown; ABR hearing tests; hair cell and SGN quantification; apoptosis assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Nontargeted metabolomics in serum and cochlea; data availability stated as available upon request; no accession number reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential pharmacological or gene therapy target to reduce serotonin overproduction and protect against aminoglycoside-induced hearing loss; suggests inhibition/knockdown strategies rather than overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Only partial rescue was achieved; systemic serotonin biology implies possible off-target neurophysiological consequences of prolonged Tph2 inhibition, not directly tested in this study
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tph1 was specifically tested and found not expressed in the mouse cochlea, supporting Tph2 as the relevant isoform in this tissue
